mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate trend over the prior 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Confirmation requires positive correlation between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward returns; refutation if no such relationship holds.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.51% as of 2018-03-01 vintage, near historically low levels post-crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign of correlation flips in more than one 3-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE recorded at 4.1% vintage, 4.0% revised as of 2018-03-01, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical UNRATE threshold-crossing instances since 2005, forward 20d SPX returns are positive rather than negative, or event count is too sparse (<10) to test in any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal broad-based consumer demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter because both consumption and durable investment channels confirm expansion.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,008M vintage vs 484,031M revised, HOUST at 1236K vintage vs 1277K revised as of early 2018
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional base rate (t-test p>0.05), or single-series RSAFS-only signal performs equally well, negating the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, providing a leading always-on signal of corporate earnings momentum that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=106.5109 vs revised_value=101.7085 as of 2018-02-01, indicating meaningful revision risk
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or the signal reverses sign after incorporating revised INDPRO values.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) below a threshold of 100,000 net jobs added signals an inflection in labor demand that precedes broader economic slowing, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly net change < 100,000 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=148,230K vs revised_value=148,280K as of 2018-03-01, showing modest upward revision
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following PAYEMS deceleration triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or fewer than 8 trigger events occur in any 5-year sample, invalidating statistical power.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than CPI inflation, the resulting increase in the real policy rate tightens financial conditions disproportionately, and this joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in FEDFUNDS exceeds 6-month annualized CPIAUCSL inflation rate
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.51% and CPIAUCSL at 249.577 revised as of March 2018, consistent with early tightening cycle
failure_condition: Real-rate interaction signal's forward 60d return distribution is not significantly different from the FEDFUNDS-only single-series signal (F-test on nested regression p>0.10), or direction flips across two consecutive tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing 4-quarter average signals approaching cyclical peak, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in slowing corporate profit growth; the effect is absent or reversed in early/mid-cycle regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below trailing 4-quarter average by more than 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=17,286.497B vs revised_value=19,882.352B as of 2017-10-01, reflecting large benchmark revision risk
failure_condition: Negative direction fails to hold (forward 60d returns positive in >55% of triggered instances) in late-cycle subsample, or the same trigger produces indistinguishable results in early-cycle regime, contradicting regime-conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: A joint short-term surge in industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with accelerating payroll (PAYEMS) growth reflects synchronized supply-side capacity expansion and labor absorption, generating a short-lived positive S&P 500 momentum effect over the next week that neither series individually predicts.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS monthly net change > 200,000 in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and PAYEMS both showed modest expansion in early 2018 vintage data
failure_condition: Joint-trigger forward 5d returns are not statistically distinguishable from returns triggered by INDPRO alone (p>0.10 on interaction term), or fewer than 15 joint-trigger events occur across the 2005-2018 sample.
